jokes.text: Is that a Star Wars reference, 'A New Beginning'? Our Next American Journey.
jokes.setup: Staffer notices the book title sounds like Star Wars
jokes.punchline: The correction reveals an equally generic, focus-grouped title
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about political book titles being interchangeably bland

jokes.text: Too late to change it? It is. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Selina realizes her book title is terrible
jokes.punchline: But it's already too late - she's stuck with it
jokes.explanation: Classic Veep cringe - being trapped by your own poor decisions

jokes.text: I call it Some New Beginnings because it's plural.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to justify her book title
jokes.punchline: Her explanation is grammatically obvious and completely unhelpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - defending the indefensible with pseudo-intellectual nonsense

jokes.text: I would hate to be that local Iowa guy that's gotta take care of her, trying to source gazpacho in a city that thinks soup is for fags.
jokes.setup: Dan imagining Selina's Iowa handler's challenges
jokes.punchline: Specific, crude observation about Iowa's cultural limitations
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about coastal elite expectations vs. heartland reality

jokes.text: Man, you guys should all marry Wendy. I feel amazing.
jokes.setup: Mike expressing his happiness about getting married
jokes.punchline: Suggests everyone should marry his wife, not get married generally
jokes.explanation: Sweet absurdist logic from Mike's genuine happiness

jokes.text: Would you like me to mold the cake into a pair of testicles for you, Gary?
jokes.setup: Gary being overly involved in wedding details
jokes.punchline: Amy's crude suggestion mocking his over-investment
jokes.explanation: Amy's typical sharp-tongued response to Gary's fussiness

jokes.text: Yeah, dream the fuck on, Dan. I'm only being nice to you because I know that Selina's gonna make me the campaign manager.
jokes.setup: Amy appearing to be friendly
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals her ulterior motive with characteristic bluntness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy - brutally honest about her ambition

jokes.text: Unless POTUS is chiseling his fucking statement out of marble, I don't get it.
jokes.setup: Dan frustrated about waiting for the President's announcement
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of literally carving the statement like ancient tablets
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - taking the delay to absurd historical proportions

jokes.text: You have more nervous tics than a shoe-bomber.
jokes.setup: Dan is acting nervous
jokes.punchline: Darkly specific comparison to a terrorist
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific Amy insult with perfect economy

jokes.text: If it melted, would it become Texas?
jokes.setup: Looking at butter sculpture of Iowa
jokes.punchline: Joke about Iowa's shape resembling Texas when melted
jokes.explanation: Decent visual joke about state shapes, economically delivered

jokes.text: You know, butter doesn't need to be refrigerated. It can be unrefrigerated or refrigerated. That's not true. I think it's like a fruit that way. No.
jokes.setup: Discussion about refrigerating the butter sculpture
jokes.punchline: Selina's completely wrong food science, immediately corrected
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina confident ignorance about basic facts

jokes.text: Well, whatever it is, I'm hearing the West Wing is abuzz like a fly in a shithouse.
jokes.setup: Reporter describing West Wing activity
jokes.punchline: Crude simile comparing political buzz to flies around feces
jokes.explanation: Colorful metaphor that fits the show's crude political atmosphere

jokes.text: Either you put your phone in the phone bowl or I put it in the punch bowl.
jokes.setup: Wendy enforcing no-phone policy at wedding
jokes.punchline: Escalating threat to destroy the phone in punch
jokes.explanation: Good escalation threat, economically delivered

jokes.text: Isn't she funny? We crack up all the time. Even when we're having sex.
jokes.setup: Mike praising Wendy's humor
jokes.punchline: TMI about their sex life that makes everyone uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike overshare - his innocent awkwardness creates cringe

jokes.text: You guys, I've been a reporter for 18 years. If anything breaks, I promise I will put it into the vows.
jokes.setup: Wendy defending keeping her phone
jokes.punchline: Absurd promise to incorporate breaking news into wedding vows
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific joke about journalist priorities trumping romance

jokes.text: It's like losing a limb. I can feel a phantom phone ringing right now.
jokes.setup: Amy giving up her phone reluctantly
jokes.punchline: Medical metaphor for phone withdrawal taken to extreme
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of phone addiction metaphor with medical terminology

jokes.text: He does bring baby wipes everywhere. You never know if we're gonna need 'em when we...
jokes.setup: Mike sharing practical marriage tip
jokes.punchline: Cutting himself off before TMI about their intimate activities
jokes.explanation: Another Mike overshare, this time catching himself mid-sentence

jokes.text: Amy, a good campaign manager always has to think one phone ahead.
jokes.setup: Dan justifying his secret spare phone
jokes.punchline: Treating redundant phone ownership like political strategy
jokes.explanation: Dan's typical pseudo-strategic justification for his behavior

jokes.text: Yes, and I likewise am disappointed that you have not. Amateur.
jokes.setup: Amy criticizing Dan's secret phone
jokes.punchline: Dan turning it around to criticize her for NOT having backup
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan move - taking the moral high ground on being sneaky

jokes.text: Hey, Richard, no offense... None taken. ...you're a catastrophe. Okay.
jokes.setup: Dan delivering an insult with false politeness
jokes.punchline: Richard's cheerful acceptance of being called a catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Richard's obliviousness makes the insult funnier

jokes.text: Then you got married. And I love that story.
jokes.setup: Wendy starting to tell a long story about meeting Mike
jokes.punchline: Selina cutting her off with fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina - pretending to care while clearly not listening

jokes.text: Oh, it's no problem. You're in Iowa, and, you know, you have to suckle Iowa.
jokes.setup: Wendy understanding why Selina couldn't attend wedding
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor about courting Iowa voters
jokes.explanation: Good political insider metaphor from journalist character

jokes.text: I feel like I'm Joe Public here. I know nothing. And I don't like that feeling.
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about being out of the loop
jokes.punchline: Vice President comparing herself to an ordinary citizen like it's degrading
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Selina's elitist perspective

jokes.text: Uh, ladies and gentlemen, the Vice President has to step out for a moment to take a phone call with a senator. He's having a big problem. It's...
jokes.setup: Gary making excuse for Selina's departure
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly trailing off mid-explanation about the senator's 'big problem'
jokes.explanation: Gary's typical awkward handling of making excuses for Selina

jokes.text: I am but a satellite caught in the gravity of your planet, Wendy.
jokes.setup: Mike reading his wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate space metaphor for love
jokes.explanation: Mike's earnest but overwrought romantic language

jokes.text: I was headed for the fall when you, Michael, gave me back my spring.
jokes.setup: Wendy reading her wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Equally overwrought seasonal metaphor
jokes.explanation: Matching Mike's overwrought style with equally cheesy metaphors

jokes.text: My official statement to the press reads thus, 'We are in love, come celebrate.'
jokes.setup: Wendy continuing her vows
jokes.punchline: Treating love declaration like a press release
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - journalist treating personal moment like work

jokes.text: We are in love, come celebrate.
jokes.setup: Reading what she claims is her 'official statement to the press'
jokes.punchline: The underwhelming official statement
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax after the overwrought vows, economical punchline

jokes.text: Stand aside, asshole.
jokes.setup: Ben arriving at hotel
jokes.punchline: Immediate crude dismissal of whoever's in his way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben entrance - maximum efficiency rudeness

jokes.text: You catch more flies with honey than with bad language. Get out of the way, or I'll fucking inhale you.
jokes.setup: Ben quoting polite saying about using honey
jokes.punchline: Immediately following with profane threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction between advice and action, with absurd 'inhale' threat

jokes.text: Do you have a preference on water? Because I'm all about Fiji.
jokes.setup: Richard offering to get Advil for Selina
jokes.punchline: Obsessing over water brand for taking pills
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard - missing the point while being earnestly helpful

jokes.text: God, he was a 4'11" stick of dynamite. A great man inside a small man.
jokes.setup: Ben eulogizing Congressman Cowgill
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment emphasizing his tiny stature
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup about the deceased congressman's size

jokes.text: Stick one of those in Ricky's hand, he would have looked like an average-sized man.
jokes.setup: Looking at hotel room lamp while discussing Cowgill's size
jokes.punchline: Suggesting lamp would make tiny congressman look normal-sized
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag using props to emphasize the height joke

jokes.text: I got a jet here that could cut a fucking diamond.
jokes.setup: Dan catching the bouquet at wedding
jokes.punchline: Crude boast about his arousal from catching it
jokes.explanation: Typical Dan crudeness, making wedding tradition sexual

jokes.text: 'Lifting the lid on the Inter-agency Softball League'? 'Face to Face with America's Wind Tycoons.' Wow. This is some seriously butter knife-dull shit, man.
jokes.setup: Dan reading WestWingMan headlines
jokes.punchline: Creative metaphor for how boring the content is
jokes.explanation: 'Butter knife-dull' is a creative way to describe boring content

jokes.text: If it ain't on WestWingMan, it ain't no thing, man.
jokes.setup: Jonah defending his gossip site
jokes.punchline: Rhyming slogan that sounds like bad marketing
jokes.explanation: Jonah's attempt at catchphrase shows his lack of sophistication

jokes.text: Now if you will excuse me, I am at a wedding, and women at a wedding are like ripe fruit ready to drop. And I am a sex wasp.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining his wedding strategy
jokes.punchline: Gross metaphor comparing himself to an insect
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah grossness - the wasp metaphor is memorably awful

jokes.text: Are you kidding? My pheromones make bitches moan. I'm gonna leave a trail right back to my apartment.
jokes.setup: Question about washing hands
jokes.punchline: Crude boast about his natural scent attracting women
jokes.explanation: More Jonah grossness, relying on crude sexual boasts

jokes.text: Look at us. You pretending to be me signing a book I didn't even write. That's politics in a nutsack.
jokes.setup: Selina observing Gary signing books for her
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate summation of political theater
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on political phoniness with memorable crude phrasing

jokes.text: It's so full of shit, there's a colon right smack-dab in the middle.
jokes.setup: Ben commenting on book title 'Our Next American Journey'
jokes.punchline: Pun on colon as punctuation vs. body part
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining grammar and scatology - peak Veep writing

jokes.text: Like, Footsteps to the Future was one. Red, White and You was actually another one. Yes Hands of Our Children.
jokes.setup: Selina listing rejected book titles
jokes.punchline: Each title getting progressively more generic and nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of terrible political book titles, especially the nonsensical last one

jokes.text: What? It's like a massacre or something. Fuckin' sick.
jokes.setup: Hearing the title 'Yes Hands of Our Children'
jokes.punchline: Ben's immediate dark interpretation
jokes.explanation: Ben's mind going to dark places makes the bad title even funnier

jokes.text: Eighteen months is an entire pregnancy with another entire pregnancy tacked onto the end of it.
jokes.setup: Ben saying 18 months is nothing for campaign
jokes.punchline: Selina's vivid metaphor making it sound interminable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that makes campaign length viscerally uncomfortable

jokes.text: You know, get my hands dirty? I could be your midwife.
jokes.setup: Ben offering to help with campaign strategy
jokes.punchline: Extending Selina's pregnancy metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the pregnancy metaphor in character

jokes.text: You know, I heard that dog picked him up and shook him really bad.
jokes.setup: Discussing Cowgill's death
jokes.punchline: Implying the tiny congressman was killed by a dog
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to height jokes, imagining absurd cause of death

jokes.text: Jonah, what's the point? You don't show up in photographs.
jokes.setup: Jonah wanting to be in group photo
jokes.punchline: Amy suggesting he's invisible or unphotogenic
jokes.explanation: Sharp Amy put-down with supernatural implications

jokes.text: Shit on my tits. Maddox just resigned.
jokes.setup: Amy reading breaking news
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude exclamation of surprise
jokes.explanation: Amy's signature crude exclamation, economically delivered

jokes.text: I hate how he learned English from pornography.
jokes.setup: Jonah using 'money shot' terminology
jokes.punchline: Amy's explanation for his inappropriate vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation explaining Jonah's inappropriate language

jokes.text: Stop flapping your vestigial limbs around.
jokes.setup: Ben telling people to calm down about Maddox
jokes.punchline: Comparing their panic to useless evolutionary remnants
jokes.explanation: Brilliant scientific metaphor for useless panic - very Ben

jokes.text: FYI, it may not be his real name.
jokes.setup: Ben wanting to find out who WestWingMan is
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious about pseudonyms with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Ben's deadpan delivery of the obvious makes it funny

jokes.text: I was voted Most Likely To Do Something Now in my class yearbook.
jokes.setup: Dan promising to take action
jokes.punchline: Fake yearbook superlative that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan - making up credentials that sound impressive but mean nothing

jokes.text: The closest you'll get to a political career will be selling Nixon masks at a Halloween shop, okay?
jokes.setup: Dan threatening Richard about keeping secrets
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific threat about political career ending
jokes.explanation: Creative specific threat that's both political and absurd

jokes.text: Just open the door while we're moving, climb under the car like Indiana Jones, pop up on the hood and write a note on the windshield.
jokes.setup: Richard asking how to turn on AC
jokes.punchline: Absurdly elaborate and dangerous solution
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation with specific action movie reference

jokes.text: I got about a half inch for you. You exaggerate.
jokes.setup: Mike talking about cake slice sizes
jokes.punchline: Wendy's response sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Accidental sexual innuendo at wedding - relies on cheap double entendre

jokes.text: You guys are just like two little pretty Easter eggs. I'd love to crack you open.
jokes.setup: Jonah approaching Amy and Dan
jokes.punchline: Gross metaphor with violent overtones
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah creepiness - trying to be flirtatious but sounding threatening

jokes.text: It's a hashtag hurricane, bitch tits.
jokes.setup: Dan asking about his blog post response
jokes.punchline: Crude boast with odd anatomical insult
jokes.explanation: Jonah trying to sound cool with crude language and weird insults

jokes.text: You don't think you should go fist a chimp?
jokes.setup: Dan suggesting he take down the post
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and inappropriate comeback
jokes.explanation: Random crude imagery typical of Jonah's poor social skills

jokes.text: What's Google's number? I don't know. Ask Jeeves.
jokes.setup: Jonah panicking about Google caching
jokes.punchline: Dan's outdated search engine reference
jokes.explanation: Good tech ignorance joke with dated reference

jokes.text: He's the bubonic fuckin' plague of this party.
jokes.setup: Ben describing Blake Stewart's effect
jokes.punchline: Comparing failed politician to medieval disease
jokes.explanation: Strong metaphor for political toxicity with historical reference

jokes.text: I kinda wish it was me in that box.
jokes.setup: Blake talking about dead Congressman Cowgill
jokes.punchline: Wishing he was dead instead
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment showing Blake's complete defeat

jokes.text: The prime minister of Scotland is on the line right now.
jokes.setup: Gary providing excuse for Selina
jokes.punchline: Scotland doesn't have a prime minister
jokes.explanation: Gary's typical bungled excuse reveals his ignorance of government

jokes.text: Leave. But I work here. Not anymore.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to enter West Wing
jokes.punchline: Stark revelation he's been fired
jokes.explanation: Economical delivery of Jonah's termination

jokes.text: There is a dead guy in the pool, and that dead guy is you.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining Jonah's situation
jokes.punchline: Mixing metaphors about being finished
jokes.explanation: Ben's mixed metaphor for being completely finished

jokes.text: The West Wing is part of my DNA and vice verse.
jokes.setup: Jonah pleading to keep his job
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'vice versa' while being dramatic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - trying to sound profound but revealing ignorance

jokes.text: That bag of wrist slits got the nomination?
jokes.setup: Selina asking about Blake Stewart
jokes.punchline: Calling him a bundle of suicide attempts
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Selina insult with perfect economy

jokes.text: You think I got here just 'cause I got $50 million in the bank and this amazing ass?
jokes.setup: Selina defending her qualifications
jokes.punchline: Listing the exact things that probably did get her there
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina self-awareness disguised as denial

jokes.text: In fact, I was gonna say 'short,' but I know how sensitive Rick was about his height.
jokes.setup: Selina beginning eulogy
jokes.punchline: Immediately referencing the dead man's sensitive topic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to running height joke in inappropriate funeral context

jokes.text: You think you've seen the end of Jonah Ryan? You haven't even seen the start of Jonah Ryan!
jokes.setup: Jonah's dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Threatening more of himself as punishment
jokes.explanation: Jonah's threat sounds more like a warning than a promise

jokes.text: I'm leaving here with my head held high and my nuts hanging low on your mom's chin, Martin.
jokes.setup: Jonah continuing his dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual insult completely undermining his dignity
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah - ruining any dignity with crude sexual humor

jokes.text: And when he got that first bite, whoa, did he reel it in.
jokes.setup: Selina using fishing metaphor in eulogy
jokes.punchline: Recycling Ben's earlier advice about being strategic
jokes.explanation: Selina recycling political advice as eulogy material

jokes.text: Jonah Ryan, 2026! That's a mid-terms year, Jonah. Well, then, I'll change it!
jokes.setup: Jonah announcing future presidential run
jokes.punchline: Not knowing election years, then promising to change democracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - complete ignorance followed by megalomaniacal solution

jokes.text: Well, I blew the fuckin' roof off of this church, didn't I? They loved that beer labels anecdote.
jokes.setup: Selina finishing eulogy
jokes.punchline: Overestimating impact of generic funeral speech
jokes.explanation: Selina's typical self-delusion about her performance

jokes.text: I'm never ambiguous, ma'am. Not even sexually.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if Ben means what she thinks
jokes.punchline: Ben's unnecessary clarification about his sexual directness
jokes.explanation: Ben's TMI moment shows his complete lack of filter

jokes.text: Well, all my orgasms have come at once.
jokes.setup: Learning Jonah is gone forever
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor for extreme satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Selina's crude expression of joy at Jonah's departure

jokes.text: Oh, Jesus Christ. I hate politics.
jokes.setup: Realizing she has more campaigning ahead
jokes.punchline: Career politician claiming to hate politics
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - politician running for president hating the game

jokes.text: This is the best day of my life!
jokes.setup: Learning Jonah is gone
jokes.punchline: Bride saying news about random person trumps her wedding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Jonah's firing overshadowing wedding day

jokes.text: I was gonna say 'baldly,' but I know how sensitive Mike is about his hair.
jokes.setup: Selina starting wedding speech
jokes.punchline: Using same joke structure as funeral speech
jokes.explanation: Selina recycling her funeral joke structure - shows her limitations

jokes.text: 'Some New Beginnings: Their Next American Journey'!
jokes.setup: Selina ending wedding toast
jokes.punchline: Shoehorning her terrible book title into the toast
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback - Selina can't help but promote her book
